title: Debian requirements : failed to locate iptables: exec: "iptables": executable file not found in $PATH
body:
Hello,

The Debian bullseye 3.0.1 podman packages have a dependency on the iptables package [1], but the testing (and unstable) version 3.4.1 has dropped this to a "suggests" [2].

We recently pulled the unstable version into our CI to work around another issue [3], and found that "podman run" started failing [4] with the error below

```
2021-10-28 03:35:56.042 | ++ podman run -d dib-work-image /bin/sh
2021-10-28 03:35:56.241 | time="2021-10-28T03:35:56Z" level=error msg="error loading cached network config: network \"podman\" not found in CNI cache"
2021-10-28 03:35:56.241 | time="2021-10-28T03:35:56Z" level=warning msg="falling back to loading from existing plugins on disk"
2021-10-28 03:35:56.249 | time="2021-10-28T03:35:56Z" level=error msg="Error tearing down partially created network namespace for container a7a992e5399d8a8537d945684ac5193b762b2dbf18f29cd3aa724c389158fb65: error removing pod cool_almeida_cool_almeida from CNI network \"podman\": could not initialize iptables protocol 0: exec: \"iptables\": executable file not found in $PATH"
2021-10-28 03:35:56.262 | Error: error configuring network namespace for container a7a992e5399d8a8537d945684ac5193b762b2dbf18f29cd3aa724c389158fb65: error adding pod cool_almeida_cool_almeida to CNI network "podman": failed to locate iptables: exec: "iptables": executable file not found in $PATH
```

After modifying the CI to also explicitly install iptables, this started working as usual.

I filed Debian bug #997976 [5] and @siretart requested I raise the question upstream, hence this issue.

We have not done any particular network configuration; this is really just a plain "podman run" that fails.

I will admit this is being run in an odd context -- we are inside a Debian Bullseye container running on Docker, and then use podman inside that container to build a container-image that we "podman export" to a .tar.gz, which we then use as a chroot for building customised images.

I feel it would be worth root-causing this so that "podman run" just works out-of-the-box for as many people as possible.

Thanks

[1] https://packages.debian.org/bullseye/podman
[2] https://packages.debian.org/bookworm/podman
[3] https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=995777
[4] https://zuul.opendev.org/t/openstack/build/144981aa53ff45a8a2aca005eeba3d86/log/nodepool/builds/test-image-0000000001.log#395
[5] https://bugs.debian.org/cgi-bin/bugreport.cgi?bug=997976

comments.body:
Podman doesn't use iptables directly, the CNI plugins do. So `containernetworking-plugins` should depend on iptables.

cc @siretart
